title: Earthquakes Lights
observation: Flashes of light or glowing orbs appearing in the sky or near the ground before or during seismic activity.
effect: Stress on crystal-rich rocks generates intense electrical charges through the piezoelectric effect.

title: Singing Sands
observation: Dunes or beaches producing a loud, low-frequency humming or booming sound when walked upon.
effect: Specific grain size and friction between sand particles create resonant acoustic vibrations.

title: Moonbows
observation: A faint, colorless, or white rainbow appearing in the sky at night under strong moonlight.
effect: Refraction of moonlight through water droplets, similar to a rainbow but with much lower light intensity.

title: Sun Pillars
observation: A vertical shaft of light extending upward from the sun during sunrise or sunset.
effect: Sunlight reflects off the surfaces of millions of horizontally oriented ice crystals suspended in the atmosphere.

title: Pingo Growth
observation: The sudden formation of a large ice-cored mound in permafrost regions, sometimes leading to a loud explosion.
effect: Subsurface water trapped under pressure expands as it freezes, forcing the ground upward.
